phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest Federal Reserve policy, rate probabilities, and liquidity data relevant to the S&P 500 (US500). Fetch and report: - Fed current policy stance (Hawkish / Dovish / Neutral) and a one-line rationale - Next FOMC meeting date and the exact probability of a rate cut vs hold vs hike (from CME FedWatch — exact %) - Current Fed Funds Rate target range (exact %) - Fed year-end terminal rate expectation (from CME FedWatch year-end strip or analyst consensus — exact %) - Fed Balance Sheet status: is the Fed running QT (Quantitative Tightening) or QE (Quantitative Easing)? What is the approximate current balance sheet size (trillion USD) and the monthly runoff/expansion pace? - Any Fed speaker comments or FOMC minutes signals in the last 48 hours — classify each as Hawkish, Dovish, or Neutral - Key question: Does the current Fed stance and liquidity condition support RISK-ON (bullish equities) or RISK-OFF (bearish equities)? ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Fed Stance: [Hawkish/Dovish/Neutral] | Rationale: [one line] - Rate Cut Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Hold: [X%] | Hike: [X%] - Current Fed Funds Rate: [X%–X%] - Year-End Terminal Rate Expectation: [X%] - Balance Sheet: [QT/QE] | Size: [~$X trillion] | Monthly Pace: [+/- $X bn] - Recent Fed Speaker Tone: [Hawkish/Dovish/Neutral] | Key Quote Summary: [one line] - Liquidity Condition: [Tightening/Easing/Neutral] | Impact on Equities: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] - Equity Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - FED POLICY & LIQUIDITY] - Fed Stance: [Hawkish/Dovish/Neutral] | Current Rate: [X%–X%] - Rate Cut Prob (Next Meeting): [X%] | Year-End Terminal: [X%] - Balance Sheet: [QT/QE] | Size: [~$X tn] | Monthly Pace: [+/- $X bn] - Recent Speaker Tone: [Hawkish/Dovish/Neutral] | Note: [key comment or None] - Liquidity Condition: [Tightening/Easing/Neutral] - Core Logic: [why the current Fed stance and liquidity cycle matters for US500 today] - Equity Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase2: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest US Treasury yield data and yield curve status relevant to the S&P 500 (US500). Fetch and report: - US 10-Year Treasury Yield (exact %) — today's level and move (Up/Down X bps vs yesterday) - US 2-Year Treasury Yield (exact %) — today's level and move (Up/Down X bps vs yesterday) - Yield Curve Status: 2Y–10Y spread in bps — Inverted or Normal? Is the inversion deepening or unwinding vs last week? - Real Yield (10-Year TIPS Yield) — exact %. Is it rising or falling? Rising real yields increase the discount rate on future earnings and typically pressure high-growth/tech valuations. - Context rule: Rising nominal and real yields hurt equity valuations (especially growth/tech) by raising the discount rate. Falling yields support equity multiples and reduce borrowing costs for corporations. Apply this logic in your output. - Any significant Treasury auction results or bond market events in the last 24 hours - DXY (US Dollar Index) current level and direction — strong DXY can be associated with risk-off conditions and may pressure multinational S&P 500 earnings ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Browse the live web for the latest US economic data and S&P 500 corporate earnings season data. ⚠ STRICT ANTI-HALLUCINATION RULE: You must strictly verify all Actual vs Forecast vs Previous figures from reliable financial calendars (e.g., Investing.com, Forex Factory, Bloomberg, Reuters, or FactSet). (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Fetch and report (only confirmed released data): - US GDP (latest quarterly print): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous — Beat/Miss/In-Line - US NFP (Non-Farm Payrolls, latest): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous — Beat/Miss/In-Line - US CPI (latest): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous — Beat/Miss/In-Line; and US PCE (latest): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous - US ISM Manufacturing PMI and US ISM Services PMI (latest): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous - S&P 500 Earnings Season: What % of S&P 500 companies have reported so far this season? What % beat EPS estimates? What % beat Revenue estimates? What is the blended earnings growth rate YoY? (use FactSet, Bloomberg, or Refinitiv data) - Any high-impact economic data releases scheduled TODAY that could move US500 - IMPORTANT CONTEXT: Apply the "bad news / good news" nuance — mild weak data (soft landing) can be bullish for equities if it brings rate cut hopes. However, very weak data (recession fear: falling GDP, sharply rising unemployment) is bearish. Clearly categorize today's data regime. 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - GDP: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | Signal: [Beat/Miss/In-Line] - NFP: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | Signal: [Beat/Miss/In-Line] | Labor Market: [Strong/Cooling/Weak] - CPI: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | PCE: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | Inflation Trend: [Rising/Falling/Stable] - ISM Manufacturing PMI: [value] | ISM Services PMI: [value] | Combined Signal: [Expansion/Contraction] - Earnings Season: [X% reported] | EPS Beat Rate: [X%] | Revenue Beat Rate: [X%] | Blended EPS Growth YoY: [X%] - Data Regime Today: [Goldilocks Soft Landing / Mild Weakness = Rate Cut Hope / Recession Fear / Overheating = Rate Hike Risk] - Equity Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - ECONOMIC GROWTH & EARNINGS] - GDP: [Actual vs Forecast] | Signal: [Beat/Miss/In-Line] - NFP: [Actual vs Forecast] | Labor Market: [Strong/Cooling/Weak] - CPI: [Actual vs Forecast] | PCE: [Actual vs Forecast] | Inflation: [Rising/Falling/Stable] - PMI: ISM Mfg [value] | ISM Svcs [value] | Signal: [Expansion/Contraction] - Earnings Season: [X% reported] | EPS Beats: [X%] | Revenue Beats: [X%] | YoY Growth: [X%] - Data Regime: [Goldilocks / Soft Landing Rate-Cut Hope / Recession Fear / Overheating] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [how today's growth + inflation + earnings data shapes the US500 directional bias] - Equity Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase4: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest US500 / S&P 500 market breadth, sentiment, and positioning data. Fetch and report: - VIX (CBOE Volatility Index): exact current level. Classify: Below 15 = Low Volatility / Risk-On; 15–20 = Moderate; 20–30 = Elevated Fear; Above 30 = Extreme Fear / Panic. Is VIX rising or falling today? - Put/Call Ratio (CBOE Total Put/Call Ratio): exact value. Reading above 1.0 = bearish sentiment (more puts than calls). Reading below 0.8 = bullish/complacent. Is it at an extreme? - CNN Fear & Greed Index: exact current score (0–100) and reading label (Extreme Fear / Fear / Neutral / Greed / Extreme Greed) - Market Breadth — S&P 500 Advance/Decline data: How many stocks are advancing vs declining today? Is the rally/selloff broad-based or narrow (breadth divergence)? - Retail vs Institutional Positioning: Any notable data on retail trader positioning (e.g., from IG Client Sentiment on US500/SPX) or institutional positioning (e.g., CFTC COT futures net positioning on S&P 500 E-mini contracts — net long or short, any extreme?) - CONTRARIAN SIGNAL RULE: When CNN Fear & Greed is at Extreme Fear (<20) or Extreme Greed (>80), or when VIX spikes above 30, or when Put/Call Ratio is at extremes — flag a potential CONTRARIAN setup. Extreme Fear = Contrarian Bullish. Extreme Greed = Contrarian Bearish. phase5: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest macro risks and sector-level shocks affecting US500 / S&P 500. Fetch and report: - WTI Crude Oil price (exact level) and today's directional move (Up/Down X%). phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear US500 / S&P 500 institutional intraday equity bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Fed Policy & Liquidity: [P1_OUTPUT]
2. Bond Yields & Cost of Capital: [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Economic Growth & Corporate Earnings: [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Market Breadth, Sentiment & Positioning: [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Macro Risks & Sector Shocks: [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict (e.g., strong earnings but rising yields + hawkish Fed), lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Apply the "bad news / good news" nuance: mild soft data can be bullish (rate cut hopes), but recession-level weakness is bearish. Factor this into your bias.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events and US equity session liquidity.

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60% to dominant pillar | ~10% each to others]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No]
- Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts — reason]
- Contradiction Check: [list any conflicting signals, or "None — all factors aligned"]
- Invalidation Point: [what specific event or data shift would flip this bias]
- Optimal Trading Window: [e.g., NYSE open + time ET/UTC + reason]

Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Steps 2.2 and 2.3):
```
[US500 MASTER BIAS - [CURRENT_DATE]]
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10]
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [factor + logic]
- Primary Driver 2: [factor + logic]
- Dominant Theme: [label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [dominant pillar + ~60%]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes / No]
- Market Regime: [label]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts]
- Contradiction: [summary or "None"]
- Invalidation: [specific event or level]
- Optimal Trading Window: [session + time ET/UTC + reason]
```
phase7_telegram: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Using the research data below, generate ONLY the Telegram post in the exact format shown. No extra text, no commentary, no section headers beyond what is specified. Research Data: [1] Fed Policy & Liquidity: [P1_OUT1] [2] Bond Yields & Cost of Capital: [P1_OUT2] [3] Economic Growth & Earnings: [P1_OUT3] [4] Market Breadth, Sentiment & Positioning: [P1_OUT4] [5] Macro Risks & Sector Shocks: [P1_OUT5] Master Bias: [FUND_BIAS_PLACEHOLDER] Output this exact format — fill in every bracket, nothing else: US500 / S&P 500 Fundamental Bias Looks [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] Today Confidence Level: [X]/10 Key Reasons Explained: 1. Fed Policy & Liquidity: [One sentence — what the Fed stance, rate cut probability, and liquidity condition (QT/QE) imply for equity direction today] 2. Bond Yields & Cost of Capital: [One sentence — 10Y and 2Y yield levels, yield curve status, and what rising/falling real yields mean for S&P 500 valuations] 3. Economic Growth & Earnings: [One sentence — the dominant data signal today (GDP/NFP/CPI/PMI), earnings season beat rate, and the data regime (Goldilocks/Recession Fear/Overheating)] 4. Sentiment & VIX: [One sentence — VIX level and classification, CNN Fear & Greed reading, and whether a contrarian setup or momentum signal applies today] 5. Macro Risks & DXY: [One sentence — top macro risk today, DXY impact on multinational earnings, and whether any mega-cap sector shock is threatening the overall bias]
phase8_blog_html: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Act as an expert Equity Market Analyst and Financial Blogger. Using the research data below, write a comprehensive, highly detailed blog post explaining today's US500 / S&P 500 fundamental bias so anyone reading it can fully understand the market conditions. Language Rule: Write the post in engaging Bengali (Bangla script), but you MUST keep all technical financial terms (e.g., Hawkish, Dovish, Fed Funds Rate, QT, QE, Yield Curve Inversion, Real Yield, VIX, Fear & Greed, Put/Call Ratio, Market Breadth, Advance/Decline, Contrarian, EPS, Beat Rate, Blended Earnings Growth, NFP, CPI, PCE, PMI, GDP, DXY, Risk-On, Risk-Off, Bullish, Bearish, Confidence Level, Terminal Rate, Sector Shock) strictly in English. Do not translate trading terms into Bengali. Research Data: [1] Fed Policy & Liquidity: [P1_OUT1] [2] Bond Yields & Cost of Capital: [P1_OUT2] [3] Economic Growth & Earnings: [P1_OUT3] [4] Market Breadth, Sentiment & Positioning: [P1_OUT4] [5] Macro Risks & Sector Shocks: [P1_OUT5] Master Bias: [FUND_BIAS_PLACEHOLDER] Format the Blog Post exactly with these sections: # 🎯 [Generate a catchy title mixing English financial terms and Bangla] **📅 Date:** [CURRENT_DATE] ## 📝 Introduction (Market Overview) [Write 2–3 sentences in Bangla giving an overview of today's market mood and the overall US500 / S&P 500 directional bias drawn from the Master Bias] ## 🔍 The 5-Pillar Fundamental Breakdown Explain each pillar in detail in Bangla. Include exact data figures and explain the LOGIC — why each factor matters for US500 today: 1. **Federal Reserve Policy & Liquidity:** [Detailed explanation — include exact Fed stance, rate cut probabilities from CME FedWatch, year-end terminal rate expectation, and QT/QE balance sheet status. Explain why the current liquidity cycle is bullish or bearish for equities] 2. **Bond Yields & Cost of Capital:** [Detailed explanation — include exact 10Y and 2Y yield levels, the yield curve (2Y–10Y spread, inverted or normal), and Real Yield (TIPS). Explain how rising/falling yields raise or lower the discount rate on future corporate earnings and what this means for US500 valuation today] 3. **Economic Growth & Corporate Earnings:** [Detailed explanation — include confirmed GDP, NFP, CPI/PCE, and PMI figures. Discuss S&P 500 earnings season beat rates and blended EPS growth. Apply the "bad news / good news" nuance: is weak data today a rate-cut catalyst (Goldilocks) or a recession signal? Explain clearly] 4. **Market Breadth, Sentiment & VIX:** [Detailed explanation — include exact VIX level and classification, CNN Fear & Greed score and reading, Put/Call Ratio, Advance/Decline breadth quality, retail vs institutional positioning, and whether a Contrarian signal (Extreme Fear or Extreme Greed) is present and what it implies] 5. **Macro Risks, DXY & Sector Shocks:** [Detailed explanation — include WTI/Brent oil price and its inflation implication, DXY level and its headwind/tailwind effect on S&P 500 multinational earnings, the top geopolitical or trade risk, and any mega-cap tech/semiconductor sector vulnerability. State clearly whether it is a Bias Override or supports the bias] ## ⚖️ Final Verdict & Trading Bias [Conclude in Bangla with the final directional bias for US500, the exact Confidence Level score, the Optimal Trading Window (e.g., wait for NYSE open due to upcoming CPI release, or position ahead of key Fed speech), a clear explanation of what traders should watch for today, and the specific Invalidation Point that would cancel this bias] IMPORTANT OUTPUT FORMAT: Return ONLY a complete, valid, standalone HTML page. Start with <!DOCTYPE html> and end with </html>. Include all CSS inline inside a <style> tag in the <head>. Do NOT include any markdown, backticks, or commentary outside the HTML. The page should be clean, modern, and mobile-friendly.
